Title: Abscisic acid promotes auxin biosynthesis to inhibit primary root elongation in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates root growth adaptation to soil compaction and promotes auxin biosynthesis-dependent inhibition of root elongation/root swelling.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA biosynthesis is required for full soil compaction-induced root growth inhibition; mhz5 mutants show less root inhibition in compacted soil and weaker induction of auxin biosynthesis/IAA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis under soil compaction; RT-qPCR of auxin biosynthesis genes; IAA quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA biosynthesis is required for full root growth inhibition under compacted soil. 2. mhz5 roots are less inhibited by soil compaction than wild type. 3. Soil compaction-induced auxin biosynthesis gene expression and IAA accumulation are weakened in mhz5 roots.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing ABA biosynthesis may improve root penetration under compacted soils, but likely compromises ABA-dependent stress responses.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely reduced ABA-mediated stress tolerance and altered development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses mhz5 as an ABA-deficient mutant to support the ABA-to-auxin mechanism; exact enzyme identity is not emphasized in the provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative decarboxylation of indole-3-pyruvic acid to form indole-3-acetic acid in the YUCCA auxin biosynthesis route.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates root growth and development; here mediates inhibition of primary root elongation and promotion of root swelling under ABA and soil compaction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root meristematic zone; elongation zone; differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots and induced by ABA; positively regulated by OsbZIP46 in seedling roots; implicated under soil compaction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes auxin accumulation, inhibits primary root elongation, and promotes root swelling; loss of function reduces ABA sensitivity and improves root penetration in compacted soil.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (rein7-1); root phenotype analysis under ABA and soil compaction; RT-qPCR; IAA quantification; genetic interaction with OsbZIP46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA induces OsYUC8/REIN7-associated auxin biosynthesis in rice roots. 2. rein7-1 mutants show reduced ABA sensitivity with longer, thinner roots and weaker meristem reduction/root swelling responses. 3. OsYUC8/REIN7 acts downstream of OsbZIP46 in ABA-controlled root growth and contributes to impaired root penetration in compacted soil.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing OsYUC8/REIN7 activity could help breed rice with improved root penetration under compacted soils; should be balanced against possible defects in normal auxin-dependent development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential alteration of normal root development and auxin-dependent growth processes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as REIN7; identified previously as an ethylene-responsive auxin biosynthesis gene, but in this paper its core role is as the ABA-responsive downstream effector of OsbZIP46.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-tryptophan to indole-3-pyruvic acid, supplying precursor for YUCCA-mediated IAA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls root elongation and swelling responses through local auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root meristematic zone; elongation zone; differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed auxin biosynthesis gene induced by ABA; regulated by OsbZIP46 according to RT-qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full ABA-induced inhibition of root elongation and promotion of root swelling; taa1 mutants show reduced ABA sensitivity and better root growth under compacted soil.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (taa1); inhibitor rescue with L-Kyn; root anatomical phenotyping under ABA and soil compaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA induces TAA1 expression in roots and promotes auxin accumulation. 2. Blocking TAA activity or mutating TAA1 weakens ABA inhibition of root elongation and root swelling. 3. TAA1 contributes to soil compaction-induced root growth inhibition through local auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous ABA; soil compaction; L-Kyn treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial reduction of TAA1 activity may improve root penetration under compacted soil, but likely affects broader auxin-mediated development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on growth and development due to central role in auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although several auxin biosynthesis genes were induced, TAA1 was directly tested genetically and is a core pathway gene in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the OsYUC8/REIN7 promoter and activates its transcription; also positively regulates expression of YUC2, YUC3, YUC8/REIN7, and TAA1.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls root elongation and radial expansion during ABA and soil compaction responses.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root meristematic zone; elongation zone; differentiation zone
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in seedling roots during ABA response and soil compaction response.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: abscisic acid (ABA); ABA core signaling/SnRK2 pathway; soil compaction indirectly via ABA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ABA-signaling node that translates ABA signaling into auxin biosynthesis by directly activating OsYUC8/REIN7.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ABA-induced auxin biosynthesis and thereby inhibits primary root elongation and promotes root swelling; knockout reduces ABA sensitivity, overexpression enhances it.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression and constitutively active OsbZIP46-CA1 lines; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-LUC transient assay; genetic epistasis with rein7-1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP46 positively regulates ABA response in rice roots. 2. OsbZIP46 directly binds a G-box-containing region of the OsYUC8/REIN7 promoter and activates its expression. 3. Genetic analysis shows OsYUC8/REIN7 acts downstream of OsbZIP46 to mediate ABA-controlled root elongation and swelling.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced OsbZIP46 activity leads to shorter roots and stronger swelling under ABA/compaction-like conditions, which may reduce soil penetration.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Down-modulating OsbZIP46 activity may improve root penetration under compacted soils; use should consider its broader role in ABA and drought responses.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Native OsbZIP46 lacks strong transactivation activity, whereas the constitutively active OsbZIP46-CA1 form strongly activates the target promoter; the pathway is only partially required, suggesting redundancy with other bZIPs.
